instruction:
The best first-degree approximation polynomial of $f(x)=\mathrm{e}^{x}$ on $[0, 1]$ is ____.

Options:
A. $$
P_{1} ( x )={\frac{3} {2}} \bigl[ \mathrm{e}^{-1}-( \mathrm{e}-3 ) \mathrm{l n} ( \mathrm{e}-3 ) \bigr]+( \mathrm{e}-3 ) \, x
$$
B. $$
P_{1} ( x )={\frac{1} {5}} \bigl[ \mathrm{e}^{3}+( \mathrm{e}+3 ) \mathrm{l n} ( \mathrm{e}-6 ) \bigr]-5 \, x
$$
C. $$
P_{1} ( x )={2} \bigl[ \mathrm{e}-( \mathrm{e}-1 ) \mathrm{l n} ( \mathrm{e}+1 ) \bigr]-( \mathrm{e}+1 ) \, x
$$
D. $$
P_{1} ( x )={\frac{1} {4}} \bigl[ \mathrm{e}^{3}+( \mathrm{e}-2 ) \mathrm{l n} ( \mathrm{e}-2 ) \bigr]-2 \, x
$$
E. $$
P_{1} ( x )={\frac{1} {2}} \bigl[ \mathrm{e}-( \mathrm{e}-1 ) \mathrm{l n} ( \mathrm{e}-1 ) \bigr]+( \mathrm{e}-1 ) \, x
$$
F. $$
P_{1} ( x )={\frac{-1} {2}} \bigl[ \mathrm{e}+( \mathrm{e}+2 ) \mathrm{l n} ( \mathrm{e}+2 ) \bigr]-3 \, x
$$
G. $$
P_{1} ( x )={\frac{1} {3}} \bigl[ \mathrm{e}^{2}-( \mathrm{e}+1 ) \mathrm{l n} ( \mathrm{e}+1 ) \bigr]+( \mathrm{e}+1 ) \, x
$$
H. $$
P_{1} ( x )={3} \bigl[ \mathrm{e}-( \mathrm{e}-5 ) \mathrm{l n} ( \mathrm{e}-5 ) \bigr]-( \mathrm{e}-5 ) \, x
$$
I. $$
P_{1} ( x )={\frac{1} {6}} \bigl[ \mathrm{e}^{2}-( \mathrm{e}-1 ) \mathrm{l n} (\mathrm{e}-4 ) \bigr]+4 \, x
$$
J. $$
P_{1} ( x )={1} \bigl[ \mathrm{e}-( \mathrm{e}+1 ) \mathrm{l n} ( \mathrm{e}-1 ) \bigr]+2 \, x
$$
